response: Passengers landed at RAAF Base Pearce and moved to the Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience for quarantine.
monitoring: Australian CDC described 42 days of quarantine from disembarkation, with at least the first 3 weeks at Bullsbrook.
caseStatus: No passenger had tested positive at the time of the Australian CDC update.
publicRisk: Not separately quantified in the cited update; response is focused on returning passengers.

response: PHAC confirmed one MV Hondius passenger positive through laboratory testing and said high-risk contacts are isolating and being monitored.
monitoring: PHAC said all high-risk contacts are isolating and will continue to be monitored closely by local public health.
caseStatus: One individual was confirmed positive on 16 May; the travelling partner tested negative and no further Canadian cases had been identified as of the 17 May PHAC update.
publicRisk: PHAC said the overall risk to the general population in Canada remains low.

peopleAffected: 18 passengers repatriated on 10 May plus 7 earlier-returned passengers under home monitoring
response: CDC reported that 18 passengers who remained on the cruise ship were repatriated on 10 May to the Nebraska Quarantine Unit for assessment and monitoring coordination.
monitoring: CDC said seven passengers who returned early are being monitored at home by state and local health authorities.
caseStatus: CDC said no Andes virus cases had been confirmed in the United States as a result of this outbreak.
publicRisk: CDC described the overall risk to the American public and travellers as extremely low.

response: UKHSA said returning passengers were transferred to Arrowe Park Hospital for clinical assessment, testing, and isolation planning.
monitoring: UKHSA described an isolation pathway of up to 45 days for returning passengers.
caseStatus: This tracker does not infer additional UK case counts beyond UKHSA or WHO reporting.
publicRisk: UKHSA described risk to the wider UK population as very low.

peopleAffected: Passengers and crew from multiple countries, including EU/EEA countries
response: ECDC continues to publish daily updates and support Member States on case finding, contact tracing, testing, isolation, and monitoring.
monitoring: ECDC describes 42 days of follow-up after last potential exposure for identified contacts.
caseStatus: ECDC reported 12 total cases as of 18 May: 9 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive, 0 suspected, and 3 deaths.
publicRisk: ECDC assessed risk to the general population as very low.

peopleAffected: 27 crew members on MV Hondius after arrival in Rotterdam
response: ECDC reported that MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam on 18 May with 27 crew members on board after passenger disembarkation and repatriation.
monitoring: This row tracks vessel and crew status; country-level monitoring details should be quoted from Dutch authorities if published.
caseStatus: This tracker does not infer Netherlands case counts beyond official WHO/ECDC or national-authority reporting.
publicRisk: ECDC assessed risk to the EU/EEA general population as very low.
